text: JANUARY - screaming and crying and flying squirrels and laughing and family and friends and tiny friends (my friends’ kids and pets 😭💕) and advocacy and protesting and hiking soup and bed chickens and lifting heavy in the gym again and so. much. snow. Also omg I’ve completed two things on my 2026 bingo already!!!
OH, WE’RE SHARING 2016 PICS?? *sprints to my old tumblr* I was fresh out of school, moody as heck, and running a mildly successful blog. Also can flower crowns come back?? I’m ready!!

Making jelly with Los Angeles lilly pillies!! Forever jealous of all the winter foraging in LA, so I’m glad I got to do a wee bit while I was there for like 48hrs 😆 also shoutout to america’s pod father @alieward for gathering berries on the red carpet with me like a true friend!!! And to @keith_lee125 for still being nice to me even when I’m a lil free fruit gremlin at events!

Was this year ROUGH? Yes. But did I also travel, get to see lots of friends and family, pick up a few new hobbies, rescue and foster two cats, see my chemical romance, be on @dropouttv twice somehow (lol some folks in the subreddit do NAWT like me 😆), raise Luna moths for the first time, lead a plant walk with my FriendHero Sam Thayer, defend native plant gardens in front of city council, make the TIME100 creators and NatGeo’s 33, make actual longform content FINALLY, take some sick bird pics, and not order delivery food for a whole year? Also yes. Also almost got chased off a mountain by bighorn sheep 🏔️😊 I’m VERY ready for 2026, but 2025 gave me some nice things to be thankful for.

WOO, FERMENTED SODAS 🫧🌲 (ALSO omg pls burp yer soda once a day once u put the lid back on lest it explode ☺️) ((also also yes, this is a throwback. in all transparency a friend of mine passed away super suddenly and im having a bit of a rough time, so i went into the archives. Hope that’s okay ❤️‍🩹))

How to make thread out of dogbane sticks! By someone who is just alright at making thread out of dogbane sticks! Feel free to critique literally any part of this! Also I’ve gotten so much fibercraft inspiration from folks like @kerstin.neumuller @eveoschaub @jillianevefa and books like “Women’s Work” by Elizabeth Wayland Barber
some thoughts about clothes from that girl who just can’t buy her food or fibers at the dang store (I debated for five whole minutes whether to use Flo Rida or Dead or Alive for this post 🎶😆 )

Happy Halloween, yall 🎃 from local woman who dresses like this everyday anyway 🩷🌱 Do I just have late medieval royal garb in my casual closet? … yes. Don’t you?? Here are some fave spooky day lewks from the last *checks watch* DECADE?? Do not make fun of 23 year old me as Ms Frizzle!!! 😆

HOW TO EAT ACORNS! This is the number one most requested video of the last month, as yall have been watching me channel my inner squirrel and gather ALL THE ACORNS 😤🐿️ If your ancestry is from the northern hemisphere, your ancestors ate acorns! But in lieu of annual grain crops like wheat, much of the globe has forgotten the practice. Here’s that lil bit of knowledge back! Feed Us With Trees by @elspethhay (currently reading) touches on our loss of perennial tree crops, and how they could be a solution to food for us that also benefits our non-human neighbors, and I highly recommend giving it a read!!

WENT TO NEW YORK FOR CLIMATE WEEK. WORE SOME CLOTHES. TOOK A TODDLER TO A THREE MICHELIN STAR RESTAURANT. HE LOVED THE HOUSE MADE SILKEN TOFU (so did i) A STRANGER PINCHED MY BUTT AFTER A DRAG SHOW AND IMMEDIATELY A GIRL GANG MATERIALIZED, READY TO KICK HIS BUTT. A REUNION WITH THE MUCH COOLER ALEXIS. I AM NOW VERY TIRED. CONCRETE JUNGLE WET DREAM TOMATOOOOOOO 💃🏾🎶
MY YEARLY CRABAPPLE PSA 🍎 (Also thank you so much for the love, yall 🩷 I really appreciate your kindness and support during this rough time for my family ❤️‍🩹)

No one tell my mom how close I was to a bear shh 🤫 GLACIER NATIONAL PARK ROUNDUP When @zoandbehold and @erininthemorning invite you to Montana, you GO! Twas a week of gorgeous vistas, amazing wildlife sightings, sore feet, and full hearts!! Also wow also don’t tell my mom I’m still really bad at taking pictures of the humans on vacation (but I’m GREAT at taking fifteen pictures of every single animal) Also omg if a long line of bighorn sheep are heading up the trail you’re heading down u should probs just run away 😅 which we did right when the impasse video cut!

BLUE MUSHROOM ALERT 🚨 meet Lactarius indigo, the indigo milkcap!! I put these in a pot with some boiling brine and the chanterelles we found today for about 6-7 mins, and put them into a jar bound for the fridge! They should keep for a month! … if I don’t eat them all first 😆

Let's snack on some wild mushrooms! These are the FRESHEST Black-Staining Polypore (Meripilus sumstinei) and Berkeley's Polypore (Bondarzewia berkeleyi) i've ever found! My lucky day! Let's make pâté! Hip hip hooray! Shoutout to @tmgastronaut for the guidance and @jbdouglas.food for putting up with me making a video during our photoshoot!

Your favorite dinosaur NEVER ate a red delicious apple… but maybe that’s for the best 😆 for WAY more exciting dino factoids catch BRAND NEW EPISODES of WALKING WITH DINOSAURS on the @pbs app! I’ve only been waiting for this for 2.5 decades 🦕

AHEM, sorry, 3.3 Million acres would be definite… 250 MILLION could be possible 🤯 According to the Wilderness Society, the data reveals that more than 250 million acres — roughly equivalent to the land mass of Texas, California and New York combined — could technically qualify for sale, despite Utah Republican Mike Lee’s claim that only “underused” parcels would be able to be sold. It’s also important to acknowledge that this wouldn’t even be able to happen if land was returned to indigenous ownership and stewardship (#landback) West coast friends, we won’t let yall down ✊🏾💚

32 had some of the highest highs and lowest lows, reminding me somewhat constantly that we only feel grief because we were lucky enough to experience love and connection. Never in my life have I felt luckier to have the friends and family I do, who are a source of so much love, light, and laughter as we all figure out this silly wonderful tragic beautiful life 💚 I’m ready for you, 33 🎂🌸☘️🐛✨

Ah, time to be a joyful killjoy 😆 ALSO there’s some debate as to whether it’s Hemlock or Cow Parsley (I still am leaning 65/35 in hemlock’s direction 🤓 I found an identical branching pattern on a Conium in my neighborhood today!) but SAFTEY is the most important thing regardless 💚🌸

HOW TO HAVE THE BEST, MOST PLANT-TASTIC TRIP TO TUCSON 💚 A huge thank you again to Dave and the whole @visittucson gang, and to @fonz_520 for being the best trip buddies a nerd could ask for! And shout out to the amazing people and businesses we were lucky enough to visit: @borderlandsplants @sanxaviercoop @flowers_and_bullets @yoliabotanica @batatucson @muralhousetucson @cerestucson @missiongarden
HOT MERMAIDWEED IS SO DANG GOOD - I still can’t believe I never knew about this lil guy until Sam Thayer introduced me to it a couple years back! It’s everywhere here!!

For millennia, foraging was a vital source of sustenance, and Alexis Nikole Nelson is helping communities reclaim the tradition. It's a practice that connects us to our ancestors and the plants they might have foraged, making it a timeless pursuit. We're proud to recognize Alexis as a #NatGeo33 honoree for sharing educational nature content and handy foraging tips with her audience. Learn more about her work and the Nat Geo 33 at the link in bio. Alexis Nikole Nelson, photographed by @djeneba.aduayom in Hundred Acre Wood Metro Park, outside Columbus, Ohio. In homage to our 33 founders, we're proud to introduce the National Geographic 33—an initiative honoring a new generation of global trailblazers who are making a profound impact on conservation, science, policy, storytelling, and beyond.

BARREL CACTUS (Ferocactus wislizeni) FRUIT TIME! And a @fonz_520 SIGHTING! It’s always a treat to visit the southwest when these tart lil fruits are in season! Part of the current and historic foodways for a MYRIAD of tribal nations like the Yaqui and the Tohono O’Odham (who’s ancestral lands I’m visiting right now with he literal angels on the @visittucson team!!), the flesh is tart and a lil slippy in that way that nopales are, too! The seeds are roasted and ground to flour (though the birds like em as is! 💚) I’m gonna try germinating a few seeds at home so I can have a little desert with me, aaaaaalllll the way in Ohio!

I AM HAVING SO MUCH FUN IN THE DESERT AND I ONLY JUST GOT HERE 😭🩷🌵 Getting to see species that I’d previously only read about is always a magical experience ✨ Add in a sunset so pretty cameras can’t fully catch it? That’s a recipe for Alexis Tears 🥹 I can’t wait to see what the rest of the week has in store!!

Want a cathartic way to give back to your local ecosystem? Try curb-stomping your invasive species! Your local flora (who compete with them for resources) and fauna (who haven’t evolved to eat or otherwise use them) will say thank you! OR! don’t! I can’t tell you what to do! I’m not your mom!

It’s delicious, nutritious stinging nettle season again!! One of my fave greens (that I also use in my hair care routine!!) and this is one of my fave ways to introduce people to them!! Cooking or breaking the lil pokey trichomes stops the sting, in case anyone was wondering! No pain, just savory goodness!
Posting this from my sickbed as this very (seemingly ETERNAL) tuft of Allium vineale taunts me outside the window 🤣 hope yall don’t mind a lil earworm throwback 🧅 🧄

Milkweed cordial! It’s finally time!! 🩷 Made with the flowers of Common Milkweed (Asclepias syriaca), this lightly fermented and fizzy beverage tastes the way milkweed smells: floral, fruity, with a dash of a warm summer’s day! I also turned some of the cordial into syrup (which I canned) so I can crack open a little bit of summer in the dead of winter 🌸✨

I found a few late bloomers on my hike today, so I had to make some cattail on le cob (name courtesy of @giantdaughter 🌾✨) the pollen is also edible, but I didn’t have a proper tasty dust receptacle on hand! 🤣

PINCH ME!!!! 🥹🫧 My FIRST EVER COLLECTION with @blueland is finally here!! It’s been a wild year and half—from smelling countless samples to picking the colors for the tin and flying all the way to North Carolina for a photoshoot in the flower meadows of Asheville, we’ve put so much love and energy into creating three limited edition scents that truly embody the joy of foraging. Foraging is more than just gathering and eating plants. It’s an act of gratitude, connection, and a reminder that it’s up to us to take care of the planet and appreciate nature’s gifts. I’m so excited to finally share this with you all and after almost two years of secrets finally get to say..... SHOP THE FORAGED COLLECTION ON www.blueland.com!!! (or use the link in my bio) 💛
My fiancé’s brother asked if I was going to see ICP so 🙃😆 I feel like I never share my weird non plant vids on here but this was too silly to not share!!

Crimson clover jelly is easy to make, and tastes so dang LOVELY! 🐝 🌺 It has a more fruity flavor profile than its very common cousins, white and red (cough cough PINK) clover, and has a song written about it! What do broccoli 🥦 , precision medicine 💊 , an atmospheric scientist 👩🏽‍🔬 and me all have in common?! We’re all a part of the Amgen Foundation’s new series, ‘But First, Science!’. Join me on this fun-filled, educational journey as we explore the impact of the Amgen Foundation through its virtual lab experiences, summer research opportunities, and state-of-the-art classroom equipment programs. The first episode is LIVE ✨today✨ on AmgenFoundation.org and features a fellow science enthusiast and biology teacher (who also happens to be a big-time science content creator), Winnie Sloan. ​ The rest of the episodes will be shared on the Amgen Foundation’s LinkedIn. Follow along to learn more about these uber-impactful programs! #AmgenFoundation #ad

POKE SALLET / SALLIT / SALAD - a dish that shows up in indigenous, african american, appalachian, and rural southern culture using pokeweed, phytolacca americana. Poisonous when raw, but thankfully its toxic components are water soluble and/or heat sensitive! I always loved the pokeweed plants in my Grammy and Grandad’s backyard, and once in passing they mentioned a dish from their younger years using the early shoots before they wete too dangerous to eat. It took until ny 20’s, after their passing, to finally have some poke sallet for myself and it felt like a little thread of connection being threaded between me and my grandparents, even though they’re gone 💚

HORSETAILS! - also while tea made from the silica-rich green shoots is consumed in many places around the world, consuming it on the regular can keep your body from processing Vitamin B1! So if you enjoy the tea, do so sparingly pls 💖💚

LILAC JELLY INGREDIENTS ✨🌟🌸 - 4 loosely packed cups of lilac flowers - OPTIONAL: a handful of violets for color - A smidge over 4 cups of water - 1 full pack of pectin (I used sure jell so I wouldn’t have to add lemon juice) - 5 cups of sugar And THANK YOU @oyveyitskay for the fantastic vegan challah!!! It was DREAMY all on its own, and with this jelly it sings!!

Virginia Bluebells, Mertensia virginica, is a spring ephemeral in the Borage family! In fact, Virginia bluebells and borage share a similar savory flavor! They truly are a magical lil guy! They emerge, put on a show, and then a few short weeks later… it’s like they were never there ✨🌱 I had fun playing around in the kitchen playing up their umami, but taming the bit of vegetal bitter (nothing crazy, not like eating a summer dandelion leaf! 🤣) The crispy leaf was based loosely off of an Indian recipe for fried Indian Borage leaves! But instead of using chickpea flour, I went for the nutty and starchy acorn flour! I know they weren’t pretty, but they were GOOD! They even passed the Geoff test 😆 Also @foragerchef has some EXCELLENT recipes on his equally excellent blog!! What spring plant should I cover next? Spring always speeds by so fast!!

Fast and easy panbread ft Cow Parsnip (Heracleum maximum!) @mallorylodonnell always does such a great job talking about Pushki (one of Cow Parsnips many names!) and is really who I credit with showing me how versatile this plant can beeeee! It’s a close relative of Golpar, but we’ll talk more about that when the seeds are in season in a few months 💖

I said I wasn’t going to make any videos while in Italy but then there was GIANT PINK SORREL (Oxalis articulata) at @nataniahoffman and @zilvinas_br’s wonderful wedding 😭💖🌸 shoutout to the beautiful moment of @fischer.tenor’s singing I managed to capture!

Measurements below if ya want em 🌸✨💖 My Thiccc Magnolia Syrup 1 cup water 1.5 cups sugar 4 cups of loosely packed, fragrant magnolia petals Magnolia Snap Cookies 3.25 cups all-purpose flour 1 cup vegan butter (like Earth Balance) 1.5 cups of brown sugar ¾ cups of My Thiccc Magnolia Syrup 1 tablespoon baking soda 1 tablespoon of vanilla extract (or Nocino if you have some lying around! I prefer it!) 2 teaspoons of cinnamon Sugar for rolling Optional: 1 tablespoon frozen grated magnolia petals (freeze the unopened flowers whole and grate as needed!) ALSO Optional: if you freshly made your syrup, save some of the sugary, jammy petals to roll into the center of the cookies!
Late Winter flower arrangement using my handy and dandy @victorinoxnorthamerica Huntsman Swiss Army Knife 🌸 💐 I love that I’m constantly prepared to forage … or floral arrange! #MadeToBePrepared #ad

When I was little I was taught to give a little sugar water to bumblebees who look tired. The unseasonably warm weather we’ve had in my neck of the woods is waking up all sorts (bees, turtles, ramps) before they necessarily have everything they need to be successful. These are the wee ones who get hit with the effects of global warming first, the canaries in the coal mine. So keep an eye out in the coming weeks for a being that could use a drink, or a little leaf litter mulch to hide from frost under. I know it doesn’t change things on a global scale (stares at multibillion dollar corporations and their emissions), but I know it mattered for the lil bee, if only for a few minutes 💖 just trying to be the best neighbor I can be

ONIONGRASS + SPORTBALL 🏈 = A TASTY TIME I made this super tasty dip using @Violifeus dairy free Cream Cheese and everyone’s favorite lil allium scamp, Oniongrass (Allium vineale!). I must confess, this recipe is bomb. This new Violife cream cheese block is bakeable and cookable which makes it versatile for any dish for all sportsball games! Click le link in bi0 for dairy free recipe swaps using Violife! Like this one! I love that this is what I get to do for my job!! happy big Sportball time to those who observe! #violifepartner PS; Fun Fact Alert: Did you know that this year’s halftime performer also really enjoys plant-based foods? He’s got great taste!

2023 had LOTS of good and also LOTS of bad, and lots of recognizing how lucky I am to have so many amazing people filled with so much love and passion and creativity in my life. My friends and loved ones keep me going, and are truly beyond compare. Every year my resolution is to be a person worthy of them all 💗here are some 2023 moments (a dump if u will) and here’s hoping I cry a lil or a lot less in 2024!

Holiday crafts are a whole heckin’ lot easier with my Huntsman Swiss Army Knife! With 15 different functions (my faves for wreath making are the scissors and the wood saw!!), I honest to goodness always have one on me when I’m out in the woods! Also HINT HINT they make a great gift! @victorinoxnorthamerica #myvictorinox #masterwithvictorinox

Most fishing films show perfection. This one shows the truth. Let’s Make a Fishing Movie pulls back the curtain on a high-stakes fishing trip with David Chang, revealing the chaos, humor, and humanity behind the camera. It’s a look at what happens when plans fall apart — and the story takes over. Starring: @davidchang @jorgepermit @mdawesjr Premiering as part of the 2026 International Fly Fishing Film Tour @flyfilmfest Find a theater near you using the link in our bio. Made possible by these epic brands: @3birdwy @thomasandthomasflyrods @abelreels @airflofishing @chumsusa @skwalafishing Filmed and produced on location at Blacksmith Lodge in Mexico @beattie_outdoor_productions @sean_mccormick @wheretowillie @majordomomedia #LetsMakeAFishingMovie #FlyFishingFilm #FlyFishing #Fishing #DavidChang

Japchae is a Korean holiday classic in my family. My mom always made it with thin beef strips. Today I’m making it vegan-friendly for some friends coming over to the studio. Why it’s a great holiday dish… • Deeply flavorful without being heavy • Colorful by design…looks great on the table • Best served at room temp • Easy to make ahead • Works for everyone • Can be gluten-free with tamari Drinking an ice-cold @athleticbrewing while I cook #ad

DAVID² 🙋🏻‍♂️🙋🏻‍♂️ Flew to LA to teach a food film course with the one and only @DavidChang at Majordomo🍊 as part of a special event hosted by @applenews & @foodandwine. The DTL kitchen got a little brighter thanks to @elmo! ❤️ Come back anytime, friend! Watch Elmo surprise @davidchang during Dinner Time Live’s 50th episode celebration right NOW only on @netflix .

50th episode of dinner time live! A celebration and Gift-mas!! Cooked some steaks, shaved some truffles, did a little pig-bladder bird situation, added a little caviar to Bianco’s pizza. Elmo didn’t seem phased. Uncle Ted gave us a bigger food budget for this one, so we tested some experiments, explored umami with Mark, and made Sean a celebratory meal with absolutely nothing spicy. After the show…Mark brought his mom’s famous chocolate chip cookies and they were demolished. So good If you missed last night’s live episode it’s steaming now on Netflix. 🙏 @seanseaevans @markrober @sesamestreet @elmo @dinnertimelive @netflix @chrisyingz @pizzeriabianco

Dear Philly, Thank you for an epic couple of days! I was in town for the @nflonprime Black Friday game and managed to catch up with the great @marcvetri (btw @vetricucinaphl is the definition of ‘worth the detour’). Had a fantastic dinner with Marc at @myloup.phl and snuck in a bite at @fiorellaphilly, which reminded me of the original noodle bar at 163 location. The persimmon with ricotta dumplings is perfection. Philly, you’re a food paradise with incredible chefs. Also had a late night cheesesteak at max’s in Germantown where I also had fantastic service! Throwback to 2018: my mom cooking, laughing, and turning Thanksgiving into an Ugly Delicious Season 2 spot. She burned the turkey every year with honey… and honestly, that was the best part. Here she is making her famous bindaeddeuk…i miss her. Hug your people today. Happy Thanksgiving.

What chef Daniel is doing at @sushi.sonagi is genuinely special. A true family operation. Parents on the fry, grill and soup stations in the back. Sister on service. Daniel channeling years of hard-earned experience from some of the world’s top kitchens. Sometimes you walk into a place that just has a pulse. That rare “they really give a shit” energy. I think I’m gonna take a page from Marie Kondo and start rating meals on one simple question: “Does it bring me intense joy?” Trying my best these days to stay away from all my usual hyperbole of “best… most delicious… next level.” Gardena for the win. Worth a detour…This place brings me intense joy. Ps. Shout out seji. IYKYK

One of my favorite restaurants in LA is @mealsbygenet. Eating here always reminds me of visiting Adam’s Morgan with my brothers and going from one Ethiopian spot to another. The flavors, the injera, the textures…they never stop amazing me. Chef Genet makes some of the most delicious food around.

Had to get shoulder surgery—rotator cuff + torn bicep… injured during the semifinals of my fantasy football league. If I’ve been MIA or changed a meeting on you the next week or two, that’s why. Thankfully, I’ve got this magical little ball of liquid happiness (aka a nerve blocker) keeping me going. Huge thanks to Dr. Gamradt and the USC Keck orthopedic surgery team for putting me back together. I’ll be back in action soon—just as soon as I can lift a spoon again.
I would runaway when my mom cooked 청국장찌개 Cheonggukjang jigae. I just couldn’t handle the intense smell. Now I crave it and my kids runaway. The cycle is complete. #uglydelicious

This was maximum pain and 💔. Just like when poor Ralph Wiggum poured his heart out to Lisa Simpson on national TV, only to get crushed—I felt exactly like poor Ralph. —————— An hour before going live on @nflonprime for the Black Friday game, I followed @rsherman25’s advice and added bacon to the Wedgebuster—the ultimate leftover Thanksgiving sandwich for the MVP of the game. We had allergy-friendly options prepped for players who weren’t fans of mayo or other ingredients…but who knew 🥓 would be the issue… or that snap to Aidan O’Connell or that sherm would sabotage me. Next time Chris jones! Shoutout to @fitzmagic14 for coming through in the clutch and being the backup wedgebuster eater. #raidernation
I would like to petition UNESCO to add the ‘Honey Bun’ to its list of ‘Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity’. It’s something I grew up eating and I love it so much.

Every day, I pack up this lunch box and send it off. We go through our routine, and it’s great. But you know what the best part of my day is? It’s those ten seconds when I open it at the end of the day, hoping it’s empty. Maybe one day, it’ll be all gone—no leftovers, no half-eaten snacks—just an empty box. I don’t know much, but I know I’m waiting for that day #lunchbox #chuckie

Messing around in kitchen…a venn diagram of: roti canai, cinnamon bun, Austrian strudel, scallion pancake, 🇰🇷 hotteuk. rock shrimp tempura with creamy spicy sauce…one of the best appetizers of all time. This is from the OG Matsuhisa but you know a dish is great when even the knock offs of the original are fantastic.

This is one of the best ideas I’ve seen in a long time. The ice cream trompo by @evil_cooks….makes a delicious ice cream taco. They are gonna keep polishing this idea and keep being different! The trumpo is in its first design right now. I can see this idea in many places…horizontal ice cream scooping….genius . Plus the temp on the ice cream is where I love it…in the process of between melted and not

Dongchimi guksu. A classic 🇰🇷 cold noodle soup. So good I had to have it two days in a row. That’s a lot of driving for a food detour. But so worth it. Korean food in the OC area is insanely good. My dad and mom would’ve loved eating here. Tastes like home. 🙏 SY!

I found out through @henrylouisgates that my dad opened a steak house with a prime rib cart…it was his first restaurant as an owner in Washington DC in the early 1970’s. Well before I was born. He never told me about any of this. Anyway cut to present day…I think the reason why I love the iconic musso’s and franks so much in LA, it makes me imagine as if I’m eating at Joe Chang’s first venture. Many of the items are the same at both restaurants. The only differences are the the prices and that i still wouldn’t like jellied consommé. Maybe one day I will bring Costin’s back to life. .85 cent martinis!!!! PS. If anyone knows anyone that ate at costin’s and remembers anything please let me know. I would love to discover more. 🙏
Super simple, very tasty dish of momofuku soy & scallion noodles with our chili crunch crossed with a loose interpretation of a carbonara. Not sure what it is exactly…but it’s delicious.

dinner: grilled marinated beef short ribs, pork belly seasoned with momofuku savory salt, jap chae, rice, jol myun, kimchi grilled in pork fat, assorted banchan with ssäm. The original animal style. ✂️

Still not pro homemade pizza …but I made this thing with leftover @momofukugoods chili crunch pork ragu and pizza dough I found in the fridge. Good things happen when you cook without a safety net. Watch the full video at the link in my bio.

Fried rice is the best breakfast…probably make this for breakfast more than anything else. Whatever is leftover in the fridge goes in - also used @momofukugoods soy, savory, and chili crunch. Watch the full video at the link in my bio.

Here is my attempt to recreate my mom’s famous 빈대떡 (bin dae duk). It’s not right…too thick and ratios are off. My mom never gave me any recipes. I know she left out ingredients or changed the order in a recipe all the time. Every time I try to make her food and it comes out worse than hers…I’ll remember my mom ❤️

My mom passed away peacefully after a 26 year battle with cancer. She so appreciated everyone’s support in her fight with cancer. She is survived by her four children and six grandkids. Our relationship with her will continue to grow. We will carry on her love, strength and kindness. I will miss her so much.

Happy Lunar New Year! 🔥🐴 Today symbolizes reunion, prosperity, and new hopes for the future. As a Vietnamese American, I’ve been celebrating this holiday my entire life and it’s a special time to be around family and to keep cultural traditions alive. After getting back from a trip to Vietnam with my family, I have a newfound appreciation for my heritage. Some of the most striking things I noticed on my trip were how hardworking the Vietnamese people are, how much they value the importance of family, and the way they pass on traditions to the next generation. Today, I’ll be celebrating with my family at home and I’m excited to start the new year with an open mind and positive outlook. May the Year of the Fire Horse bring joy and new opportunities to us all! @lexarmemory #LexarMemory #LunarNewYear #tet #yearofthehorse #yearofthefirehorse

As a travel photographer, I travel far and wide to find new sources of inspiration, but sometimes the best sources of inspiration are sitting right there in front of you. AI will never be a replacement for the incredible moments I’ve been able to experience in this life. There are those moments in life you just know you’ll always remember. Seeing the northern lights dancing across the night sky will be a core memory that will forever live rent free in my mind. What’s a moment in your life that you’ll never forget? #northernlights #auroras #canada #thisisnotai #bucketlist

I don’t care what anyone says, this will always be one of my favorite cities in the US. If I could live in any other city besides Los Angeles, it would definitely be San Francisco. Every time I visit this charming city, I’m already thinking of planning another trip. With endless views of the Golden Gate Bridge, a never ending list of new places to eat, and plenty of places for my pup to roam, what’s not to love? 🌁 #sanfrancisco #bayarea #goldengatebridge #sf #norcal

Believing that making a jacket that’s marginally less bad for the environment is a mechanism to stop global warming is akin to believing that the solution to alcoholism is drinking light beer. I wrote about my time lurking in Ventura and working Patagonia and how and why I reached this conclusion on my substack, link in bio.

Over three years in the making, the short film that @moviemountain made for @rivianofficial is finally out. Out of all the commercial work I’ve worked on, this is my favorite one, by far. The sets were massive, the truck was over 3 feet long. I’ll post later this week about the process but here’s the film in its entirety. Thanks to @3rd_larry for thinking that Gear Guard could come to life via miniatures and stop motion.

Some talking point ideas for family dinners, down time and post meal walks this holiday season, courtesy of the PROFESSOR @moviemountain. In order of appearance, Terrence McKenna, “Hot” Carl Sagan and Neil Tyson.

22.2.22 happy birthday to the most handsome dog oskar -I always grew up wanting a dog.. when I got older and experienced losses I was scared when my family got a dog that I have another being in my life I have to be scared of losing one day. This might sound weird but I felt like I had no more emotional capability to welcome someone/thing into my life where I know our time is limited.. but now I’m so happy we have him. I miss him so much even though I never even grew up with him. He’s a part of our family and he’s brought and brings so much joy into our lives and im so endlessly grateful for little Oskar

@ninaskalikova and I connected over Instagram last year… then I get a text “Hey Rida, I love your profile, I’m coming to Bangalore, I’m a photographer…want to do a shoot together?” And of course I said YES. When we finally met…she was one of the most gorgeous women I’ve ever laid my eyes on. Kind, sweet, warm… we spent the day walking around, clicking pictures, and now I finally have them. I love these shots because it’s how she sees me through her lens. We’ve stayed connected ever since, and I can’t wait for her to come back to India… or for me to land up where she is next. . Staying on the move has always been a big part of my independence. Being able to go where I want, when I want, without having to depend on anyone else is a freedom I never take for granted. And that’s why I love having options like @uber_india . Whether it’s getting to work, meeting friends, or heading out for something spontaneous, it just makes moving around so much easier. This Women’s Day, here’s to celebrating independence in every form and to platforms that help women stay on the move. Because when women move forward, the world moves with them. 💛
